description_long: This hammer features a robust, rectangular wooden head with a natural grain pattern and visible cracks, suggesting it is crafted from a solid block of wood. The head is reinforced with two wide, black metal bands that wrap around its sides, likely to prevent splitting and add weight for effective striking. The handle is made of lighter-colored wood, smoothly finished, and slightly curved for ergonomic grip, with visible wood grain running along its length. The striking face of the hammer is flat and broad, while the opposite side is squared off, lacking any claw or peen features. Metal rods protrude from the head, possibly for additional reinforcement or as part of the construction. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible branding or decorative elements, and the hammer appears to be in good condition with no significant wear or damage.
description: A heavy-duty wooden hammer with a rectangular head reinforced by black metal bands and a smooth, curved wooden handle. The hammer is utilitarian, with a flat striking face and no visible branding or damage.
description_view_0: Side view showing the rectangular wooden head, two black metal bands, and the full length of the curved wooden handle.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the flat striking face, wood grain, and cracks in the head, with the handle extending backward.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the squared-off back of the head, metal bands, and the handle's attachment point.
description_view_3: Top-down view revealing the circular wood grain pattern, cracks, and the arrangement of metal rods and bands.
attribute_handle_type: curved wooden handle with visible grain, smooth finish, and ergonomic shape
attribute_head_type: rectangular wooden head reinforced with black metal bands, flat striking face, no claw or peen
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: visible cracks in the wooden head, otherwise none visible
